sections.paragraph:
(Milêl), from the root נוּחַ, f.-

(1) a letting down; Job 36:16, נַחַת שֻׁלְחָֽנךָ “food set down upon thy table;” Isa 30:30, נַחַת זְרֹועֹו “the letting down of his arm,” i.e. the punishment of his arm (compare Psa 38:3).

(2) rest, Isa 30:15 Ecc 6:5. Acc. Ecc 4:6, מְלֹא כַף נַחַת “a handful in quiet.”

(3) [Nahath], pr.n.; see תֹּוחַ .
